kadayf-dessert-with-walnuts-lezzet.name: Kadayif Dessert With Walnuts
instructions: 1. Put the dough in small parts and share it to the bottom of the small muffin containers.
2. Press on it, ensure that the dough is well placed into the molds.
3. Wait for 1 night room heat to dry.
4. Take the butter from the jealous fire and rub it from the strainer.
5. Pour melted butter with spoon on dried calves and bake about 15 minutes until pre-heated 180 degree bakeries in the oven.
6. Wait for 5 minutes from the oven.
7. Place the bowls of dough from moulds to servis service plate.
8. To remove dust sugar and water from the pot and boil in the middle fire.
9. Add lemon juice and boil for 10 minutes.
10. Taste with orange juice and add internal walnuts and cook for a few minutes.
11. 11. Fill in the dough with the help of the walnut mixture spoon and serve it after cooling.

kale-with-the-greenest-tahini-sauce.name: Kale With The Greenest Tahini Sauce

ingredients.item: Pinch of aleppo pepper or hot paprika

ingredients.prep: stems removed, torn into bite-size pieces

instructions: Ingredient Info: Aleppo pepper, a slightly sweet Syrian pepper with a moderate heat level, is available at some specialty foods stores and wholespice.com.

Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add garlic and Aleppo pepper and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds. Add kale and 1 Tbsp. water. Cook, tossing, until kale is wilted and tender, about 3 minutes. Toss with tahini sauce, season with salt, and serve with lemon wedges.

karyns-cream-of-crab-soup.name: Karyn's Cream Of Crab Soup
instructions: Blend flour and hollandaise sauce mix with 2 cups half-and-half in a medium bowl.

Melt butter in a medium, heavy saucepan over medium heat. Slowly add hollandaise mixture, stirring constantly until thickened. Add remaining half-and-half, whipping cream, seafood seasoning, dry mustard, and celery seed. Bring to a simmer and reduce heat to low.
Add crabmeat and sherry, stirring gently until warmed through. Serve immediately.

kebab-with-peppers-and-cherry-tomatoes-a.name: Kebab With Peppers And Cherry Tomatoes

ingredients.item: 1 red onion cut into thin rings
1 red onion cut into thin rings

instructions: We can instead cook the Florine peppers, use ready peppers in a jar.
We drag them well.
Add all the actors for the kebab in a bowl and mix them very well.
Allow the mix to freeze for 1 hour.
We split up in six places.
We form six pieces.
We need six slabs for kebabs.
We touch every souvli on the mix.
Wet our hands in cold water and start dressing with the palm.
We make sure every kebab is long and thin.
Bake for 4 minutes on each side.
We serve right away.

keto-sheet-pan-chicken-dinner.name: Keto Sheet Pan Chicken Dinner

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat the oven to 218°C (220 degrees C). Line a 12x18-inch rimmed baking sheet with aluminum foil.
Combine garlic powder, onion powder, and black pepper in a bowl. Sprinkle over both sides of chicken tenders. Wrap each tender with a slice of bacon and set in the middle of the baking sheet.
Place halved asparagus spears on one side of the chicken. Season with salt and pepper to taste and drizzle with 1 tablespoon of oil.
Place riced cauliflower on the other side of the chicken and season with salt and pepper to taste. Drizzle with remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil.
Roast in the preheated oven for 13 to 15 minutes, stirring cauliflower and asparagus halfway through.

Set oven to broil and cook until bacon is browned and sizzling and chicken is no longer pink in the center, an additional 2 to 3 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 74°C (74 degrees C).
Remove from oven and drizzle tenders with hot sauce. Serve immediately, or divide into storage containers and refrigerate.

kimchi-creamed-collard-greens.name: Kimchi Creamed Collard Greens
instructions: Place a large pot over medium heat and add the olive oil. Add the onion and sauté until it has some color. Add the bacon and cook for 10 minutes, or until fully rendered.

Add the collards and sauté until a bit limp, 4 to 5 minutes. Add the vinegar and cook down until almost dry. Add the sorghum, stock, 2 cups of water, and kosher salt to taste. Simmer, covered, for about 45 minutes, until the collards are tender.

While the collards are cooking, get the kimchi cream ready to roll: In a small saucepan, cook the cream over medium heat, watching it closely, until it has reduced by one-third, 4 to 5 minutes. Add the chopped kimchi.
When the collards are tender, stir the kimchi cream into the greens and cook to marry the flavors, about 10 minutes. Serve.

kookoo-sabzi.name: Kookoo Sabzi

ingredients.prep: picked through, soaked for 10 minutes and drained
or a few fresh leaves, finely chopped (optional)
or 1 sprig of fresh tarragon, leaves chopped (optional)

instructions: Working in batches, finely pulse the greens in a food processor, until finely chopped but not mushy. Alternatively, use a sharp knife and large cutting board. Set the greens in a large bowl. Finely chop the green onions. You can do this in the food processor, but take care (green onions quickly turn mushy). Add the green onions and the rest of the ingredients except for the eggs and the oil to the green herbs and give a stir to combine. Add the eggs and mix well to combine. The batter should have the consistency of thick yogurt or soft-serve ice cream.

In a large (10- or 12-inch) nonstick frying pan with a lid, heat 1⁄3 cup of the olive oil over medium heat. Add the batter, and spread evenly. Cook the kookoo until the oil starts to bubble along the sides, about 3 minutes. Cover and cook until the kookoo starts to set, and the bottom is browned, 12 to 15 minutes. Cut the kookoo evenly into 4 large pieces, and using a wide spatula flip each piece over one at a time. You can also set a dish beside you, take one piece of kookoo out to make room, flip the other pieces, and place the piece back in. Drizzle 2 tablespoons of oil in between all the cuts, reduce the heat to medium-low, and cook, uncovered, until cooked through, about 10 minutes. Cut the kookoo into desired pieces and serve warm or at room temperature.
Prepare up to 3 days in advance. Serve at room temperature or gently heat through in the oven.

kulakli-with-minced-meat-argiro.name: Kulaki With Minced Meat

ingredients.item: Flour as long as it takes
Flour as long as it takes

instructions: Mix the ingredients for the dough and add flower as long as it takes until it is made.
Open the dough into a Leaf, which is cut into square small pieces.
Mix the minced meat with the egg, pepper, salt, parsley and some oil.
Then, place a teaspoon mixture of fined meat in each of the pieces of dough and close it like a bow.
Place them in a baking pan which has previously been ground with oil and baking them at 180
The kulakli is accompanied with tzatziki.
